title: galarhoeus micranthus
intro: Galarhoeus micranthus, also known as G. micranthus, is a type of plant that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family, commonly referred to as the cashew or poison ivy family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Galarhoeus micranthus include its potential in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of Galarhoeus micranthus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine.
